runtime.description: Identifies the execution environment. Known values: 'claude-api' (direct Anthropic API call managed by the runner), 'claude-code' (Claude Code CLI, invoked as a subprocess), 'python' (Python script), 'kanecta-function' (executes a Kanecta function item via kanecta-sdk — config.functionId required), 'group-chat' (structured multi-agent deliberation between config.participants — config.participants required). Open string — new runtimes can be added without spec changes; the runner resolves runtime names to adapter implementations.

model.description: Model identifier for API-based runtimes (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-6', 'claude-opus-4-8'). Null for runtimes that manage model selection themselves ('claude-code', 'group-chat'). When null and the runtime requires a model, the runner applies its configured default.

systemPrompt.description: Base system prompt defining this agent's identity, role, and general behaviour. Combined with the pipeline phase instructions at run time — the system prompt sets up who the agent is; the phase instructions set up what it should do. For 'group-chat' runtime this is the facilitator prompt — it frames the conversation and rules of engagement; each participant agent's own systemPrompt drives their individual voice. Null defers to the runtime's default system prompt.

tools.description: Array of MCP tool names this agent can access. Restricting tools is a safety measure. For 'group-chat' agents, include 'kanecta_conclude_discussion' if using terminationCondition 'explicit'. Null means all tools available in the current MCP context are accessible.

config.description: Runtime-specific configuration. Structure is determined by the runtime adapter. Null means runtime defaults apply.

max_tokens.description: claude-api: maximum tokens in the model response.

working_directory.description: claude-code: working directory for the Claude Code subprocess.

env.description: claude-code: environment variables injected into the subprocess.

script_path.description: python: path to the Python script to execute.

interpreter.description: python: Python interpreter path (e.g. 'python3', '/usr/bin/python3.11'). Defaults to 'python3'.

functionId.description: kanecta-function: UUID of the Kanecta function item to execute.

participants.description: group-chat: UUIDs of the agent items that participate in the conversation. Agents take turns in round-robin order. Each participant's own systemPrompt and tools are used for their turns.

maxTurns.description: group-chat: hard cap on total turns across all participants. Applies regardless of terminationCondition — prevents runaway conversations. Default 10.

terminationCondition.description: group-chat: how the conversation ends. 'explicit' — continues until any participant calls kanecta_conclude_discussion; that participant writes the final items. 'max-turns' — runs for maxTurns turns then outputAgent synthesises and writes final items.

outputAgent.description: group-chat: UUID of the agent re-invoked to synthesise and write final items after 'max-turns' termination. Null for 'explicit' termination where the concluding participant is the writer.
